summary: Applied the private platform-context API and Qdrant runtime on docker-runtime-lv3, exposed it through the Proxmox host Tailscale proxy on port 8010, and rebuilt the live retrieval corpus with normalized repo-path citations.

verification.observed: `make converge-rag-context` completed successfully after hardening the role for build-only images, offline-safe embeddings, mirrored corpus path normalization, and Docker nat-chain recovery before published-port recreates.

`curl -sS -m 20 -D - http://100.118.189.95:8010/healthz` returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK` with `{"status":"ok","collection":"platform_context"}` after `make converge-guest-network-policy` opened host-to-guest port `8010` on `docker-runtime-lv3`.

`python3 scripts/query_platform_context.py --api-url http://100.118.189.95:8010 --api-token-file .local/platform-context/api-token.txt --question "how does step-ca issue SSH certificates" --top-k 3` returned cited matches including `docs/adr/0042-step-ca-for-ssh-and-internal-tls.md` and `docs/runbooks/rag-platform-context.md`.
`python3 scripts/build_rag_index.py --dry-run` reported `source_count: 150` and `chunk_count: 923` from the repository corpus.

notes: The deployed runtime now uses the offline-safe `token-hash` embedding backend on this platform because outbound Hugging Face model downloads are not reliably reachable from docker-runtime-lv3.
Guest-network-policy convergence had to allow `10.10.10.1 -> 10.10.10.20:8010` so the Proxmox host socket proxy could reach the private API on the runtime VM.
